page.title: Celebration in the House of Eternity

revision.text:
[[de:Feier im Haus der Ewigkeit]] '''Celebration in the House of Eternity''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox Egyptian Hieroglyphs Two Column |NAME = W3-G17-pr-I10:X1-N16
Heb-em-per-djet
''ḥb-m-pr-ḏ.t''
''Festival in the House of Eternal Time'' /
''Festival in the House of Eternity'' |NAME_EXTENDED = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q177819}} }} The '''Festival in the {{ill|House of Eternity (Ancient Egypt)|en|House of Eternity (Ancient Egypt)|qid=Q1590754|lt=House of Eternity (Ancient Egypt)|1=House of Eternity (Ancient Egypt)}}''' was an important {{ill|Ritual|en|Ritual|qid=Q189819|lt=ritual|1=Ritual}} in the {{ill|Mortuary cult|en|Mortuary cult|qid=Q2445700|lt=mortuary cult|1=Mortuary cult}} of {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}}. By the term "House of Eternity," the Egyptians meant the tomb complex that the tomb owner built during his lifetime for his "repeating life in the {{ill|Afterlife|en|Afterlife|qid=Q623282|lt=afterlife|1=Afterlife}}." The Festival in the House of Eternity arose from {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|qid=Q205740|lt=Ancient Egyptian mythology|1=Egyptian mythology}}, according to which special annual festivals were held to commemorate the dead, stemming from concerns about nourishing the deceased and their {{ill|Soul|en|Soul|qid=Q9165|lt=souls|1=Soul}}. == Festival in the House of Eternity == === Offerings for the Dead === Throughout Ancient Egyptian history, an extensive {{ill|Cult (religious practice)|en|Cult (religious practice)|qid=Q756820|lt=cult|1=Cult (religious practice)}} of offerings for the dead developed over millennia, governing the relationship between the living and the deceased. The associated magical spells, {{ill|transfigurations|de|Verklärung (Altes Ägypten)|ja|変容 (古代エジプト)|zh|古埃及蜕变|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/transfigurations|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Prayer|en|Prayer|qid=Q40953|lt=prayers|1=Prayer}}, solemn rites conducted by priests and family, {{ill|False door|en|False door|qid=Q902996|lt=false doors|1=False door}}, {{ill|Statue|en|Statue|qid=Q179700|lt=statues|1=Statue}}, {{ill|Sacrifice|en|Sacrifice|qid=Q179723|lt=offering tables|1=Sacrifice}}, and grave goods were unique in both their extent and duration compared to other {{ill|Religion|en|Religion|qid=Q9174|lt=religions|1=Religion}}. The cult of offerings for the dead already existed in the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q177819}}. Initially, the provisioning of food and drinks primarily aimed at the daily nourishment of deceased kings and deities. From the {{ill|Pyramid|en|Pyramid|qid=Q12516|lt=pyramids|1=Pyramid}} of the {{ill|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q269237|lt=Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Fifth Dynasty of Egypt}} and the {{ill|Sixth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Sixth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q269979|lt=Sixth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Sixth Dynasty of Egypt}}, offering spells and rituals have been preserved that formed the foundation of later variants of mortuary cults. In this context, the {{ill|Wag Festival|de|Totenfest des Osiris|1=Festival of Osiris|1=Wag Festival|2=de|3=Totenfest des Osiris|1=Festival of Osiris|4=fr|5=Fête Ouag|6=ja|7=オシリスの死者祭|8=zh|9=奥西里斯死亡节|lt=Wag Festival (Festival of the Death of Osiris)|ja|ワグ祭|zh|瓦格节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wag Festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of Osiris|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Thoth Festival|de|Thot-Fest|ja|トト祭|zh|托特节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thoth Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} probably emerged during the Old Kingdom, although their roots trace back to the {{ill|Thinite Period|de|Thinitenzeit|ja|ティニス時代|zh|提尼斯时期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thinite Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, particularly regarding royal burials conducted in {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|qid=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt|1=Abydos, Egypt}}. Later, festivals of individual {{ill|Necropolis|en|Necropolis|qid=Q200141|lt=necropolis|1=Necropolis}} cities emerged, where the local deity visited the necropolis. This included the festival {{ill|Pulling of the Sokar-barge|de|Ziehen der Sokarbarke|ja|ソカルの船の牽引|zh|索卡船祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pulling of the Sokar-barge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, celebrated widely throughout Ancient Egypt. However, the best-known was the {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|en|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qid=Q478205|lt=Valley Festival|1=Beautiful Festival of the Valley}}, initiated during the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} under {{ill|Mentuhotep II|en|Mentuhotep II|qid=Q296439|lt=Mentuhotep II|1=Mentuhotep II}}. === Feast and Festivities === Depictions from the Old Kingdom already show tomb owners celebrating feasts with family and friends. Music and dance formed a special part of these festivities. With the advent of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}}, a notable shift in traditional rites became evident, as the loosening of established forms in the {{ill|Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q146055|lt=Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt}} brought a new liveliness to celebrations. [[File:The judgement of the dead in the presence of Osiris.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Judgement (afterlife)|en|Judgement (afterlife)|qid=Q1236322|lt=Weighing of the Heart|1=Judgement (afterlife)}} ({{ill|British Museum|en|British Museum|qid=Q6373|lt=British Museum|1=British Museum}})]] During the reign of {{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|qid=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III|1=Thutmose III}}, images appeared that combined depictions of {{ill|burnt offerings (Ancient Egypt)|de|Brandopfer (Altes Ägypten)|ja|燔祭 (古代エジプト)|zh|燔祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burnt offerings (Ancient Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with the "arrival of temple singers" and the festival of "{{ill|Bringing the Bouquets|de|Bringen der Sträuße|ja|花束を捧げる祭|zh|献花节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bringing the Bouquets|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Concurrently, some traditional rites disappeared, such as the old transfigurations emphasizing the deceased’s ascent as {{ill|Aku|en|Aku|qid=Q416611|lt=Akh|1=Aku}} spirits. The introduction of the {{ill|Book of the Dead|en|Book of the Dead|qid=Q174361|lt=Book of the Dead|1=Book of the Dead}} provided broader segments of the population the opportunity to enter the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|qid=Q871389|lt=Duat|1=Duat}} after death, shifting the focus towards secular blessings for the living tomb owner. This new development became prominent in later funerary celebrations. The festivals during the reign of {{ill|Amenhotep II|en|Amenhotep II|qid=Q158052|lt=Amenhotep II|1=Amenhotep II}} represented the peak of lively and informal rites. This informality led to later overpainting in tombs from Amenhotep II's period during the {{ill|Nineteenth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Nineteenth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q157956|lt=Nineteenth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Nineteenth Dynasty of Egypt}}, as naked serving girls in tomb images were retrospectively clothed, deemed "{{ill|Puritanism#Practice|de|Puritanismus#Praxis|ja|清教主義#実践|zh|清教主义#实践|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Puritanism#Practice|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." == Literature == * {{ill|Jan Assmann|en|Jan Assmann|qid=Q61247|lt=Jan Assmann|1=Jan Assmann}}: ''Tod und Jenseits im Alten Ägypten.'' Beck, Munich 2003, ISBN 3-406-49707-1. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491464|1=Siegfried Schott}}: ''Das schöne Fest vom Wüstentale.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften, Mainz 1953. {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar|qq=}} ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|de|Feier im Haus der Ewigkeit|version=201348463|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:永遠の家の祭]] [[de:Feier im Haus der Ewigkeit]] {{wikidata link|Q1401414}}
